sections.paragraph:
δίλογος, ον (Pollux 2, 118, but=‘repeating’. Likew. Diod S 20, 37, 1 διλογεῖν τι=say someth. twice; cp. Nägeli 52) insincere (cp. our ‘double-talking’ [in the U.S.A. ‘double-tongued’ may convey a different mng.]) 1 Ti 3:8.—Pol 5:2.—B 5:4. M-M.

Pollux Pollux , II a.d.—List 5

Diod S Diod S , I b.c.—List 5

someth. someth. = something

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

mng. mng. = meaning(s)

Pol Pol = Polycarp to the Philippians—List 1

B B = Barnabas (the Letter of), II a.d., except in series of uncial witnesses, in which case B refers to Codex Vaticanus (s. also Vat.). When the abbrv. B would ambiguous, Vat. is used for the codex.—List 1

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 250
